items.title: Perth almost created a City of South Park in 2014 — here's why it might again

children.content: In 2015 the Barnett government abandoned plans to merge Perth councils — but now the idea has reared its head again. Here's what you need to know.

items.title: As coral reefs are wiped out from bleaching, this one brings hope

image.altText: A coral reef with a fish swimming in the centre.

children.content: In the midst of a mass coral bleaching event along the West Australian coast, a group of scientists have discovered a section of reef that has been able to withstand the heat.

items.title: Last private midwife north of Perth to close as costs of business rise

children.content: Soon-to-be parents in regional WA will have less choice over where and how they give birth as care options diminish, midwives say.

items.title: After losing four relatives to dementia, woman shares family's story

children.content: Gwen Knox, from Western Australia, has written a play inspired by her family that she hopes starts important conversations about dementia and truth-telling.

items.title: WA gas users warn Labor not to repeat flaws in state reservation scheme

children.content: Western Australian gas users are urging the Albanese government to build stronger transparency and enforcement into its proposed national gas reserve scheme.

items.title: Truck torn in half after collision with train at level crossing, six injured

children.content: Train services in Perth return to normal after at least six people, including two toddlers, are injured when a train collides with a truck at a level crossing in the city's east.

items.title: 'I'm over it': Minister lashes City of Perth over 'diabolical' culture

children.content: The "diabolical" City of Perth should be merged with its neighbouring council, a WA government minister has claimed, describing the "pettiness, infighting, immaturity" by elected members as "galling."

items.title: Former Nationals candidate refused bail over alleged hit and run

children.content: The 26-year-old allegedly struck the victim with his Toyota Hilux as he was leaving for work, causing 'catastrophic' head injuries.

items.title: Rinehart-backed rare earths mine gets green light to test China's dominance

children.content: Arafura Rare Earths has announced its flagship project north of Alice Springs will go ahead, decades after it was first flagged as a prospect.

image.altText: a drone shot of a mining camp in Central Australia.

items.title: A mouse plague has broken out, here's what you need to know

children.content: Mice in their millions are eating grain crops, destroying food, and invading houses. Here's how it all started.

articles.title: Stronger mouse poison allowed for 'worst plague on record'

children.content: Farm groups have welcomed the chemical regulator's long-awaited decision to approve double-strength mouse baits amid plague numbers in Western Australia and South Australia.

items.title: How Australia's EV uptake compares with the rest of the world

children.content: High petrol prices are driving EV sales around the world, with the leading international authority predicting 30 per cent of all new cars sold this year will be electric.

items.title: Albanese's hard sell as budget opponents line up

children.content: For a group of people who declared they were prepared to tackle a post-budget scare campaign, the government has looked anything but in control this week.

image.altText: Anthony Albanese and Jim Chalmers walk through the doors at parliament house.

items.title: Man's NDIS payments allegedly spent by homesharer on holidays, massages

children.content: A 70-year-old appointed homesharer faces court charged with 264 fraud offences after allegedly accessing the bank account of an intellectually disabled man who was living with him and spending over $54,000.

image.altText: A cllose-up shot of a man with short grey hair and a moustache, wearing spectacles and a dark polo shirt, outside court

items.title: Results are out for the world's most powerful company, but investors aren't impressed

children.content: The world's most valuable company, tech giant Nvidia, has reported a record revenue but investors are not impressed with second-quarter sales forecasts.

image.altText: Nvidia logo and rising stock graph

items.title: WA's idled nickel plants to come back to life to process gold

children.content: Idle infrastructure from WA's once-booming nickel industry is set to be retrofitted to produce gold bars amid historic high prices for the precious metal.

items.title: Coles investigates 'weird scratching' Apple gift card scam complaint

children.content: Coles is looking into a Melbourne mother's complaint about faulty Apple gift cards worth $2,000.

items.title: Gen Zs who approach share market like 'lottery ticket' could pay more tax

children.content: Gen Zs who take a lottery ticket approach to investing in the stock market will likely pay more tax under the capital gains tax changes being proposed by the government.

items.title: Kylie Minogue reveals 'secret' cancer and IVF struggles in Netflix doco

children.content: "Our Kylie" gets candid in the three-part Netflix docuseries, grieving over IVF struggles, Michael Hutchence, and revealing she was diagnosed with cancer a second time in 2021.

items.title: Australian parents in 'survival mode all the time' break taboos on raising kids

children.content: As the birth rate hits a record low it's been revealed that almost 60 per cent of Australian parents are experiencing psychological distress, according to the largest-ever national parenting survey.

items.title: Fears mount for victims of domestic violence ahead of WA court closures

children.content: The doors of 26 regional and remote courthouses in WA will shut for three months, leaving lawyers confused and concerned about consequences for domestic violence legal matters.

articles.title: Balgo residents will now have to travel 11 hours to go to court

children.content: In this remote East Kimberley community, where the local court has been shut for almost two years due to security concerns, the closure of the nearest regional court from June 1 is raising concerns about justice.

items.title: Pressure builds on new homes and inflation as costs go through the roof

children.content: Amid fast-rising prices — from materials craned in, to rubbish trucked out — a residential builder lists the costlier surcharges, levies and products hammering the construction industry and fuelling inflation.

items.title: Can Australia make its own fertiliser without fossil fuels?

children.content: Most fertiliser is a by-product of the oil industry, which is why it's so vulnerable to the blockade of the Strait of Hormuz. But there are other sources the industry could eventually switch to.

items.title: El Niño looms as BOM releases long-range winter forecast

children.content: The Bureau of Meteorology is forecasting winter to be unusually warm across the entire country as an El Niño climate pattern is increasingly likely to take hold.

items.title: Father in critical condition as police probe whether altercation with hoons led to assault

children.content: Geraldton police are investigating whether complaints about hooning were behind an alleged assault that left a father-of-three in a critical condition.
